topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my screen, a jarring intrusion into the quiet afternoon. My breath hitched. It was him. Mark. The name alone sent a cold tremor down my spine. I stared at the profile picture—a smug grin I remembered from middle school, years ago. A wave of heat rushed up my neck, making my palms sweat. How could he even think to reach out now? I felt a tightening in my chest, a sharp, unpleasant clenching. I hesitated for a long moment, my thumb hovering over the 'Accept' button. Every instinct screamed at me to block, to delete the request, but some part of me was paralyzed, weighing the awkwardness against the sheer audacity of the gesture.

[story 2]

When the alert chimed, I nearly dropped my phone. My knuckles went white where I gripped the device. Mark. Of course. I could practically feel the familiar knot forming in my stomach, a tight, unpleasant squeeze. I immediately minimized the messaging app, shoving my chair back so hard it scraped across the hardwood floor. My jaw tightened, and I could feel the muscles in my neck tense. *What is he trying to do?* The thought echoed loudly in my head. I paced the room twice, the sound of my own footsteps seeming deafening. The urge to lash out, to send a scathing reply filled me with a burning resentment. I took a deep, shaky breath, trying to force my expression into something neutral, though my lips felt brittle.

[story 3]

I glanced at the screen, and a slow, burning flush crept up my cheeks. It was him. The sheer unexpectedness of it made my stomach churn with a sour feeling. I didn't move for several seconds, just staring at the digital invitation. A low growl started in my throat, a sound I immediately tried to swallow down. I felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to slam my laptop shut, to physically sever the connection. My hands clenched into tight fists at my sides, the tension radiating outward. When I finally did tap 'Accept,' the action felt heavy, reluctant, like dragging a lead weight across the floor. I could feel my whole body vibrating with suppressed irritation.
